policy: Patterns teach structure and strategy only.
F/S/stepdown/tool diameter come from current Neo4j context.
Current user geometry and origin override examples.
Every candidate must pass local geometry invariants and validate_gcode.
Current tool scope is END_MILL_ONLY; circular holes are circular milling features, not drilling operations.

algorithm: Convert requested finished bounds to cutter-center bounds using tool radius.
For lower-left origin: X=r..W-r, Y=r..H-r.
For center origin: X=-W/2+r..W/2-r, Y=-H/2+r..H/2-r.
Split total depth into passes no larger than Neo4j depth_max.
Use zigzag lanes with stepover near 0.6D, then finish the boundary.
Retract to safe Z between depth passes.
invariants: finished swept envelope equals requested W×H
anti_patterns: using machine travel as pocket size
comparing centerline span directly to finished size

algorithm: Resolve circle center from origin: center=(0,0) for center origin or (R,R) for lower-left bounding-box origin.
Maximum cutter-center radius is pocket_radius-tool_radius.
Split depth by Neo4j depth_max.
Cut concentric G2/G3 full circles with radial stepover near 0.6D.
Use I/J relative center offsets; final swept diameter equals requested diameter.
optimization_priorities: avoid polygonal approximation when controller supports G2/G3

algorithm: Choose the longer slot dimension as the primary cutting direction.
Offset centerline limits by tool radius.
Use bounded cross-step lanes; do not repeatedly retract inside one depth pass.
Split depth by Neo4j depth_max and finish side boundaries.
invariants: slot swept envelope equals requested dimensions
anti_patterns: treating slot as one centerline when width > D

applicability: contour side explicitly inside or outside
algorithm: Compute nominal finished rectangle around lower-left or center origin.
Offset cutter center inward for inside contour or outward for outside contour by tool radius.
Use a closed four-edge loop per depth pass.
Do not guess compensation side.

patterns.strategy: helical circular interpolation with end mill
algorithm: Use a cutter-center radius of (feature_diameter-tool_diameter)/2.
Split axial depth by operation-specific Neo4j depth_max.
Enter with G2/G3 helical interpolation; never emit G81-G89.
Finish with a full circular interpolation at final depth.
anti_patterns: vertical plunge when tool capability forbids it

patterns.strategy: feature-scoped blocks selected by teacher loop
applicability: all required feature roles, extents and depths resolved
algorithm: Freeze the accepted FeatureGraph and hash it.
Generate at least two bounded candidate strategies.
Tag every operation block with feature ID/type/operation/depth.
Run END_MILL_ONLY, static, feature coverage, depth-map and topology validation.
Rank only fully valid candidates.
